claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 5 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 5 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 5 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you some questions.

First, could you please tell me: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I have one child. I'd like to know if I'm eligible to claim Child Benefit for them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you a few more questions about your child and your circumstances.

**How old is your child?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you. **Does your child currently live with you in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, they live with me here in the UK. We're in Birmingham.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you. Just a couple more questions to complete the assessment.

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for this child?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. One final question:

**What is your child's name?** (I need this to complete the assessment)

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good news! **You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Based on the information you've provided:
- Alex is 5 years old (under 16)
- Alex lives with you in the UK
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex

You meet all the standard eligibility criteria. You normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK, and you're usually considered responsible for a child if you live with them.

**Please note:** If you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may have to pay a High Income Child Benefit tax charge. However, this doesn't affect your eligibility to claim - you can still claim and receive the benefit, but may need to pay some or all of it back through your tax return.

You can proceed with making a claim for Child Benefit for Alex.

reasonings: Alex is 5 years old (under 16), lives with the claimant in the UK (Birmingham), and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for them. According to the rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK. You'll usually be responsible for a child if you live with them. All eligibility criteria are met for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex, who is 5 years old and lives with them in the UK. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. The claimant meets all the standard eligibility criteria for Child Benefit.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 5 years old (under 16), lives with the claimant in the UK (Birmingham), and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for them. According to the rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK. You'll usually be responsible for a child if you live with them. All eligibility criteria are met for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex, who is 5 years old and lives with them in the UK. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. The claimant meets all the standard eligibility criteria for Child Benefit.
